producer.name: Bodegas Lustau
producer.philosophy: One of the most respected sherry houses in Jerez de la Frontera, renowned for its Almacenista program — bottling exceptional soleras from small, independent stockholders. Founded by José Ruiz-Berdejo, a lawyer and notary who stored his wines in his office courtyard. Four historic bodegas: Las Cruces, Emperatriz Eugenia, La Campana, and El Aljibe. The range spans every sherry style from bone-dry biologically aged Finos to intensely sweet sun-dried Pedro Ximénez.

wines.name: Fino Jarana
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Palomino Fino from albariza soils. Fortified to 15% ABV and aged under a veil of flor yeast in the solera system at Bodega Las Cruces. Average age approximately 4 years. The flor protects the wine from oxidation, preserving pale color and developing characteristic yeasty, almond aromas.

wines.name: Manzanilla Papirusa
wines.style: Manzanilla — dry, biologically aged in Sanlúcar
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Palomino Fino aged under flor in Sanlúcar de Barrameda, where the cooler, more humid Atlantic climate produces a thicker, more active flor veil than in Jerez. This gives Manzanilla its distinctly lighter, more saline character compared to Fino. Average age approximately 4 years in solera.

wines.name: Amontillado Los Arcos
wines.style: Amontillado — dry, combined biological and oxidative aging
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Palomino Fino. Begins life as a Fino under flor, then fortified further to kill the flor and continue aging oxidatively. Equal periods under biological and oxidative aging, giving it the pungent nose of a Fino with the nutty depth of an Oloroso. Average age approximately 8 years. Aged at Bodega Emperatriz Eugenia.

wines.name: Oloroso Don Nuño
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Palomino Fino. Fortified to 17%+ ABV from the start, preventing flor development entirely. Ages purely oxidatively in the solera at Bodega La Campana, developing rich walnut, tobacco, and dark chocolate aromas. Average age approximately 12 years. Despite its intense color and concentration, this is a dry wine.

wines.name: Palo Cortado Península
wines.style: Palo Cortado — dry, rare style between Amontillado and Oloroso
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Palomino Fino. The rarest sherry style — originally classified as Fino, but the flor died unexpectedly, leading the wine into oxidative aging. Combines the aromatic finesse and pungency of an Amontillado with the body, richness, and structure of an Oloroso. Average age approximately 12 years. Aged at Bodega Emperatriz Eugenia.

wines.name: Pedro Ximénez San Emilio
wines.style: Pedro Ximénez — intensely sweet dessert sherry
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Pedro Ximénez grapes, sun-dried on esparto grass mats (asoleo) after harvest until they are practically raisins. This concentrates sugars to extreme levels before the brief fermentation and fortification. Aged oxidatively in solera for approximately 12 years, developing flavors of fig, date, coffee, and dark caramel.

wines.name: East India Solera
wines.style: Cream Sherry — sweet, oxidatively aged blend
wines.vinification_notes: A blend of old Oloroso and Pedro Ximénez, referencing the historic practice of shipping sherries to the East Indies and back — the tropical heat and motion accelerating the aging. This modern interpretation achieves similar richness through extended solera aging. Sweet and concentrated with dried fruit and spice complexity.

wines.name: Oloroso Pata de Gallina
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Palomino Fino from the private solera of almacenista García Jarana, bottled by Lustau. Only 38 casks in this exclusive solera at Bodega El Aljibe. 'Pata de Gallina' (chicken foot) indicates wines with above-average glycerol levels, giving exceptional richness and smoothness. Average age approximately 15 years. Lustau pioneered the almacenista concept — selecting and bottling exceptional sherries from small, independent stockholders.

wines.name: Amontillado del Puerto
wines.style: Almacenista Amontillado — dry, single-stockholder solera from El Puerto
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Palomino Fino from the private solera of almacenista José Luis González Obregón in El Puerto de Santa María. Approximately 10 years average age with extended biological aging followed by oxidative maturation. The maritime influence of El Puerto imparts a distinctive saline quality. Bottled by Lustau as part of the Almacenista range.

wines.name: Fino de Jerez 3 en Rama
wines.style: En Rama Fino — unfiltered, drawn straight from cask
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Palomino Fino bottled 'en rama' — directly from the cask with minimal or no filtration, clarification, or stabilization. Released in seasonal sacas (typically spring) when the flor is at its most vigorous. This preserves the full aromatic intensity and textural complexity that is lost in standard filtered bottlings. Aged approximately 4 years in American oak casks in the solera system.
